steps.title: Identify x-intercepts as solutions to f(x) = 0
steps.content: An x-intercept occurs where the graph crosses the x-axis, meaning where $y = 0$. Set the function equal to zero: $$0 = (x+6)(x-4)$$

steps.title: Apply the zero product property
steps.content:
The function is already factored. By the zero product property, if a product equals zero, at least one factor must equal zero.

Set each factor to zero: $$x + 6 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad x - 4 = 0$$ Solving: $$x = -6 \quad \text{or} \quad x = 4$$ The x-intercepts are at $x = -6$ and $x = 4$, so the x-coordinate of an x-intercept is $-6$.
